transcript.text: Be the sales guy telling them what I heard.
want them to be able to, like, basically deliver much more effectively by being able to reference the call.

Yeah, so I would say the primary early lead volume was all from inbound sources, so our series A launch and word of mouth.

Now I would say the majority of our new pipeline actually comes from cold outbound, which is great, and we're excited that we have a team that can operate and build a really effective cold outbound channel.
But we just know how much harder it is to close outbound leads than inbound folks.
And we know content needs to be an increasing investment of our time, and it's just that we just don't have the bandwidth here to do it.
I'm the only person that even pseudo-handled anything growth-related because we've been lucky enough to get a lot of lead volume, and I don't even work in growth.
I work on my product team.

So I think the primary focus of us is going, like, how do we bring on, like, a somewhat white-glove vendor here to act as an interim growth content marketing lead to just help us kind of get everything.
We're very well-versed in how to spin up effective SEO landpages and all that kind of stuff, but we don't have the manpower to actually do it effectively right now.
So trying to bring on an external vendor or a trusted agency to hopefully kind of bring us from zero to one in that stage.

And I just realized it's not the right solution for us.
And I'm honestly a little too nitpicky about the fine-grained detail on the SEO side for those to be the right vendor.
I need someone that's like, even if it's a little more of an expensive option, like a white glove solution, here's the content calendar we're going to do.
I'm going to be your full-time content marketer for X period of time to help us start scaling.
If that goes well, I would love to kind of scale it up to a larger partnership, but that's kind what we're looking for.

mean, we know Jason, so I have a pretty good idea that I think you guys are in the right space as a vendor here.

And then once we've built like a good enough surface area, then we can start thinking about programmatic as like the second work stream that we would move to.
But I just want to make sure that's like aligned to what you guys need at the end of the day.
Yeah, sounds like a good start.
My only concern with like editorial pieces is just how picky our team, especially our leadership, is on stuff that goes out publicly under our name.
And I don't want it to make it where it's like we're spending just as much time communicating with you all and getting that stuff out than we would just doing it in-house.
I do think like from my perspective for editorial type content, like 80% of the lift is the idea, the outline, and the like images or collateral.
And about 20% of the work is actually writing it.
Like we can...
I could have a freelancer do it with some GPT and be just fine for cheap.
So I just want to make it so it's not like we feel like we're still providing that 80% and what you guys are doing is doing some GPT polish and putting it live.
Does that make sense?
So I do think editorial is a good approach.
I also know that you guys have programmatic solutions that I think could be interesting.
We're also here, and this is the part where I don't know as much about how much you guys do on stuff that's not just pure editorial-type, blog-type content.
If you guys do more targeted lead magnets, making guides for lead magnets, other kind of stuff along those lines.
And then sometimes some of the lame nuance, like meta-titles, meta-descriptions, interlinking, acquiring a strong, healthy, backlink profile, what your guys' program looks like there.
But if was just editorial blogs, I'd be curious to see how much of a white glove service that really is versus how much of it we respond daily in Slack.
And then at that point, it just feels like I could do that with a freelancer for both of you.
Does that make sense?
Yeah.
Yeah, totally.

the templates here are meant for you to open up in product as emails.

this is a little different.

Enterously like, yeah, I don't think this would be the primary focus.

Where do we want to build topical authority?

cases.

so that's, you know, that in addition to all the SEO analysis, the roadmap of assignments that we would work on in phase two, that's the other key piece of the partnership.

Other pieces that we might have in the knowledge base would come from resources that we can ingest directly from our clients.

I can answer any questions.
That you have, but in short, we built all these workflows in code.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Evaluate GrowthX as a content marketing partner for Conversion.ai.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=13.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**The Need:** Conversion.ai requires a "white-glove" agency to build an inbound channel, as its current cold outbound lead source is harder to close.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=13.0)
  - [**The Offer:** GrowthX proposed a $15k/mo editorial service, starting with an 8-week, $30k strategy sprint that includes publishing ≥10 articles.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1029.0)
  - [**The Hurdle:** A 2–3 month kickoff delay (first availability is late Jan 2026) conflicts with Conversion.ai's urgent need to "put the pedal to the gas."](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1431.0)
  - [**Next Step:** Kyle will review GrowthX case studies (with SEMrush data) to validate quality and ROI before deciding if the wait is acceptable.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1483.0)

## Topics

### Conversion.ai's Challenge

  - [**Problem:** Over-reliance on cold outbound leads, which are harder to close than inbound.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=13.0)
  - [**Goal:** Build an inbound channel via content marketing, but lacks the internal bandwidth.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=13.0)
  - [**Requirement:** A "white-glove" partner to act as an interim growth/content lead, not a low-cost point solution.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=48.0)
  - [**Content Priorities:**](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=127.0)
      - [**Editorial:** High-quality blog content for SEO and topical authority.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=127.0)
      - [**Programmatic:** Landing pages, lead magnets, and sales collateral.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=192.0)
      - [**Technical SEO:** Interlinking, meta-data, and backlink acquisition.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=216.0)

### GrowthX's Proposed Solution

  - [**Service Model:** A managed editorial service, starting with a 3–6 month engagement before expanding to programmatic work.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=572.0)
  - [**Process:** End-to-end service (strategy → publishing) powered by an internal platform and human experts (MD, Editor, Manager).](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)
  - [**Pricing:**](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1029.0)
      - [**Strategy Sprint:** $30k for 8 weeks.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1029.0)
          - [**Deliverables:** SEO analysis, content roadmap, and ≥10 published articles.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1029.0)
          - [**Rationale:** Secures commitment for the upfront strategic investment.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1144.0)
      - [**Ongoing:** $15k/month for daily publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1029.0)
  - [**Workflow:**](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1169.0)
      - [**Inputs:** Client resources (decks, calls) and deep research (Exa, Tavli) feed AI-powered workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1280.0)
      - [**Outputs:** Includes internal/external linking, images, and direct CMS publishing (Webflow).](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1358.0)

### The Scheduling Conflict

  - [**GrowthX's Availability:** Booked through EOY 2025; first kickoff is late Jan 2026.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1431.0)
  - [**Conversion.ai's Urgency:** The 2–3 month delay conflicts with leadership pressure to accelerate growth.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1462.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:** Send Kyle case studies with SEMrush data and specific article examples for a quality spot-check.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1483.0)
  - [**Kyle:** Review materials to validate quality and ROI, then decide if the Jan 2026 start date is acceptable.](https://fathom.video/share/h6-gdpEBciAhwoSztcU9W4vQmDXbsJBs?tab=summary&timestamp=1483.0)

action_items.description: Email Kyle case study w/ SEMrush graph + exact article URLs; Kyle to review/spot-check
